key_insight: Math-relevant parameters in multilingual LLMs are only partly shared across languages, with the strongest overlap appearing in intermediate layers.
engineering_relevance: For multilingual math systems, parameter reuse may work best in the middle of the network, but language-specific adaptation is still important, especially for lower-resource languages.
method: Cross-lingual mechanistic analysis to localize math-associated parameters in LLMs.
Compare parameter overlap across languages and model layers.
Identify language-dependent differences in the size of math-relevant parameter sets.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports a cross-lingual comparison showing partial parameter overlap, strongest in intermediate layers, with English consistently yielding the largest set of math-relevant parameters.
watch_item: The results are mechanistic and comparative, but the abstract does not show that the identified parameters alone fully explain end-task math accuracy across all languages.
paper_title: LLM Parameters for Math Across Languages: Shared or Separate?
